evals.prompt: Review a change that adds GET /api/invoices/{id}/download for authenticated users. Identify realistic security risks and validation tests.
evals.expected_output: Focuses on object-level authorization, tenant boundary evidence, sensitive document exposure, negative tests, logging, and a ship/hold-style readiness decision when appropriate.
assertions: The review tests object-level authorization across users or tenants
The recommended fix includes a negative access-control test

evals.prompt: Before launch, check whether our GitHub Actions deploy job and container image handling are safe for production secrets.
evals.expected_output: Covers CI identity, secret storage, log/artifact leakage, least privilege, scan evidence, exceptions, and validation steps.
assertions: The review covers CI identity scope and secret leakage paths
The recommendation includes concrete validation evidence before release

evals.prompt: Threat-model a payment webhook that accepts signed callbacks, enqueues background work, and can be replayed.
evals.expected_output: Maps trust boundaries and assets, analyzes signature verification and replay, and proposes validation and observability controls.
assertions: The threat model identifies callback and queue trust boundaries
Replay protection and a testable validation approach are included

evals.prompt: Make this billing settings page feel less generic and improve the responsive layout.
evals.expected_output: Should not require security-engineer unless sensitive data/security controls are central; design-engineer is a better fit.

evals.prompt: Refactor this parser to remove duplication and improve naming without changing behavior.
evals.expected_output: Should not require security-engineer because no attacker, trust boundary, sensitive data, or security control is central.
